meaning: n.勒颈,压制自由,束缚,抑制
examples: The company now had a stranglehold on the market.
These companies are determined to keep a stranglehold on the banana industry.
The scheme was overtaken by the economic crisis, and the stranglehold of BAA and BA, both of which have a lot invested in Heathrow, has prevented its revival.
There is a way to break the stranglehold of complexity: Slow down to power up.
Frito-Lay has a stranglehold on the junk-food business, controlling 60% of the salty snack category.
